content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Note maturity lifecycle prevents summary-store drift summary=The architecture defines a note maturity lifecycle (`fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`) so `Note` memory remains curated instead of degrading into a summary-store; maturity is raised by reuse, multi-source support, non-conflict with new data, time stability without supersede, and recurring rationale relevance. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=Note maturity,fresh,stabilizing,mature,consolidated,summary-store tags=memory_lifecycle,note_quality,doc_section_digest evidence=Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Memory repair is a distinct workflow from consolidation summary=`Memory repair` is defined as a separate background workflow focused on memory health issues (duplicates, contradictions, profile drift, stale tasks, bad merges, note spam, retrieval budget leakage), while consolidation has a different role of enriching memory; they are explicitly different jobs. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 14. Memory repair' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<1> keywords=Memory repair,consolidation,background workflow,duplicate notes,contradictory active items tags=repair_workflow,memory_health,doc_section_digest evidence=Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`v3` memory architecture uses four axes to avoid brittle or dump-like memory summary=In `v3`, memory is organized along four axes—Representation (Source/Claim/Note/Profile/Task/Episode), Lifecycle (write→reconcile→consolidate→repair→retain/archive/delete), Retrieval policy (claim-first/note-first/mixed/evidence-first with bounded budgets), and Scope & lineage (scope_json/scope_text/source refs/origin note/memory runs). The document states all four are necessary to prevent transcri...[truncated 44 chars] scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=v3,four axes,representation lifecycle retrieval scope lineage tags=v3,architecture,memory_model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Update path is not append-only and requires retrieval/reconciliation summary=The architecture defines memory writes as an update workflow, not blind append: decide whether to write, retrieve similar/conflicting items, produce typed candidates, and apply policies (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes quality degradation such as duplicates, stale preferences, conflicts, task explosion, and summary spam. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=append-only,retrieval-before-update,reconciliation,policy tags=architecture,memory-write-path,doc_section_9 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended pipeline routes routed writes through canonicalization, specialized updaters, reconciliation, and storage summary=In the recommended architecture, Memory Router outcomes (noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed) feed Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer, then specialized components (Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, Note Constructor) and reconcilers write to Postgres + pgvector, with background consolidation/repair/retention workers and runtime retrieval-answer loop. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory Router,Entity Canonicalizer,Postgres + pgvector,Read-Time Retrieval Planner tags=pipeline,recommended_architecture,component_roles useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Truth state and retrieval state are separate axes summary=The document requires keeping truth status separate from retrieval temperature: truth state tracks epistemic validity (with different allowed values for claims vs notes), while retrieval state tracks hot-path/search handling; items can be resolved or superseded yet remain warm for rationale or historical evidence. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=truth state,retrieval state,warm,superseded,resolved tags=state_model,retrieval_policy,architecture_doc useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.72,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Distinct section-level concept (note maturity lifecycle, promotion/demotion signals) not captured in existing active notes; it refines the broader architecture overview.","target_note_id":null},{"action":"insert","candidate_index":1,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.69,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"New reusable guidance that repair is a separate workflow from consolidation with explicit responsibilities; no direct duplicate among active notes.","target_note_id":null}]}
